2003-09-01.title: The Arms Dispute That Has Hurt Blair's Credibility Has Wounded the BBC, Too
2003-09-01.abstract: May 29 statement on BBC radio broadcast by defense correspondent Andrew Gilligan that British government probably knew its claim that Saddam Hussein had biological and chemical weapons deployable within 45 minutes was false before it inserted it into its dossier of intelligence on Iraqi arms has produced polarizing battle between BBC and Prime Min Tony Blair's government; battle is being played out in current high profile probe into apparent suicide of Dr David Kelly, Defense Ministry weapons expert who was source of report; most attention has been focused on decline in public trust in Blair, but BBC's ratings have slid as its practices and internal judgments have been exposed; BBC chairman Gavyn Davies complains of attack on BBC impartiality and integrity; government may have reacted with added vigor because Gilligan later wrote column for tabloid with anti-Blair bent contending Alastair Campbell, Blair's powerful director of communications, forced 45-minute claim into document over security services' objections; Campbell responded by using vast media apparatus at his command to denounce BBC; photo (M)

2003-09-01.title: Libya to Inflate Amount Paid in '89 Bombing
2003-09-01.abstract: Libya reportedly agrees to increase compensation for 1989 bombing of UTA airliner over Niger that killed 170 people; $34 million Libya paid to France in 1999 after Paris court convicted six Libyans in absentia for attack became issue after Libya agreed to $2.7 billion package for bombing Pan Am jet in 1988 over Lockerbie, Scotland; France has threatened to block lifting of United Nations sanctions against Libya unless its victims get comparable compensation (M)

2003-09-01.title: Iraqi Shiites Flex Muscle Even as They Mourn
2003-09-01.abstract: Funeral cortege for slain Ayatollah Muhammad Bakr al-Hakim crawls south from Baghdad in tumultuous cavalcade freighted with both political and religious symbolism as Iraq's Shiite Muslims assert their importance while mouring one of their leading voices; tens of thousands of mourners march through city and line route south in first day of three-day rite; United States military says it will delay turning over control of area around Najaf to Polish-led forces following bombing at city's holiest shrine that killed Hakim; Iraqi Governing Council meets to agree on final list of cabinet members; American officials say they hope move will let Iraqis begin to take over responsibilities from Americans; photos (M)

2003-09-01.title: Kakata Journal; An Army of Educators Saves a Liberian College
2003-09-01.abstract: Teachers and overseers at Booker T Washington Institute, Kakata, Liberia, have stood guard around the clock for past 16 months to protect Liberia's sole survinging institution of higher learning from latest cycle of civil war violence; school was founded with American support in 1929, run by black and white Americans into 1960's, and many of its graduates live in United States; map; photo (M)

2003-09-02.title: North Korea Says It Will Continue Nuclear Talks
2003-09-02.abstract: The reversal came as Chinese officials asserted that the bigger obstacle to a diplomatic solution was U.S. reluctance to begin bargaining in earnest.

2003-09-02.title: World Briefing | Asia: Afghanistan: 7 Afghans Die In Taliban Attacks
2003-09-02.abstract: Taliban-led attacks kill 7 Afghan police and soliders on Kabul-Kandahar highway, disrupting road reconstruction efforts (S)

2003-09-02.title: Libya and Families in Accord On Payment in '89 Bombing
2003-09-02.abstract: France says Libya and families of 170 people killed in terrorist attack on French UTA airliner in 1989 have reached tentative compensation agreement after pledge by Col Muammar el-Qaddafi to increase payments; French government has not been directly involved in negotiations, but Pres Jacques Chirac intervened, with telephone call to Qaddafi, to underscore seriousness of matter in eyes of French government; families of victims were enraged when Libya agreed to pay up to $10 million for each victim of bombing of Pan Am airliner over Lockerbie, Scotland; original settlement for victims of bombing of UTA jet was less than $200,000 each (M)

2003-09-02.title: Smaller Raids by U.S.
2003-09-02.abstract: A new series of raids in northern Iraq against supporters of Saddam Hussein's government is the first wide-scale use of an American strategy of carrying out smaller, more precise attacks, based on better intelligence, commanders say.    Article, Page A11.

2003-09-02.title: Emotional Farewell to Iraqi Shiite Cleric Slain by Car Bomb
2003-09-02.abstract: NAJAF, Iraq, Sept. 2 — The central streets of this holy city convulsed with a final spasm of mourning today as Ayatollah Muhammad Bakr al-Hakim, the assassinated cleric who had promised to deliver Iraq's Shiites from repression, was buried in a large empty field expected to be transformed into a shrine.

2003-09-03.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Iraq: World Church Group Wants U.S. Out
2003-09-03.abstract: World Council of Churches, meeting in Geneva (Switzerland), urges American and coalition forces to withdraw from Iraq, turn over control to United Nations and pay reparations to Iraqi people for war damage to country (S)

2003-09-03.title: Expulsion of Arafat Threatened by Israeli
2003-09-03.abstract: Defense Minister Shaul Mofaz said that Israel might move to expel the Palestinian leader, Yasir Arafat, by the end of the year.    ''I believe that he has to disappear from the stage of history,'' Mr. Mofaz told Israel's Army Radio, adding that Israel ''made a historic mistake by not expelling him some two years ago.''

2003-09-03.title: BUSH LOOKS TO U.N. TO SHARE BURDEN ON TROOPS IN IRAQ
2003-09-03.abstract: Bush administration officials say Pres Bush agreed at meeting with State Sec Colin L Powell to begin talks in United Nations Security Council to authorize multinational force for Iraq, but insisted that troops be under American command; decision, key strategy shift, is tacit admission that US-dominated force is stretched too thin; comes as Congressional Budget Office study shows Army lacks active-duty troops to keep current occupation force in Iraq past next March, without extra help from other services, reserves or other nations or without vastly expanding its size; official says Bush envisions withdrawing most American forces within 18 months to two years, to be replaced by UN peacekeeping force (M)

2003-09-03.title: Train Bombing Kills 5 and Hurts 30 Near Chechnya
2003-09-03.abstract: At least five people were killed and more than 30 were injured today when two bombs exploded under a passing commuter train near Chechnya.

2003-09-03.title: Africans Outdo U.S. Patients In Following AIDS Therapy
2003-09-03.abstract: Evidence is emerging that AIDS patients in Africa are better at following their pill regimens than Americans are; undermines prejudice that incomplete pill-taking in Africa could speed mutation of drug-resistant strains; surveys in Botswana, Uganda, Senegal and South Africa find that, on average, AIDS patients take about 90 percent of their drugs, compared to 70 percent in United States; compliance has become easier becuse drugmakers from India and elsewhere are making triple-therapy drugs that come in as few as two pills a day (M)

2003-09-04.title: Study Finds Europeans Distrustful of U.S. Global Leadership
2003-09-04.abstract: New poll finds Europeans are increasingly uncomfortable with United States' global leadership, with majorities in almost every nation opposing direction of American foreign policy; survey, conducted by German Marshall Fund of US and Compagnia di San Paolo of Italy, finds difference between American and European attitudes widening as longtime allies of US increasingly chafe under American leadership; changing attitudes date to Bush administration's decision to wage war in Iraq without authorization of United Nations Security Council (M)

2003-09-04.title: A Jog in Baghdad Offers Lessons About Life in a War Zone
2003-09-04.abstract: First-person account by reporter Dexter Filkins of life in Baghdad; says city has grown menacing and bleak; says bombings of recent days have given life heavy feel; says that, added to heat and haze, make it unhappy time for American soldiers stationed there; says he has taken to jogging at night, to amazement of onlooking Iraqis; photo (M)

2003-09-04.title: Hussein Tape Is Authentic, C.I.A. Says
2003-09-04.abstract: Central Intelligence Agency believes that Saddam Hussein was probably the speaker on new audiotape broadcast on Arab television on Sept 1; on tape, speaker denies that Hussein or his followers were behind bombing in Najaf that killed top Shiite Muslim cleric and almost hundred others (S)

2003-09-04.title: Marines Turn Over a South-Central Area of Iraq to a Polish-Led International Force
2003-09-04.abstract: United States hands over command of area of south-central Iraq to Poland's military, but American marines hold onto Najaf, major religious seat within area, for at least two weeks to try to ensure stability in city; incomplete transfer serves as flawed model for multinational cooperation US hopes to amplify; in Baghdad, members of Iraqi cabinet take their oaths of office; map (M)

2003-09-04.title: Helpers to Get Seat at Table, Rumsfeld Says
2003-09-04.abstract: Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld says countries that contribute troops and financial support to American-led military and reconstruction operations in Iraq could count on seat at table in decision making about mission there; says US hopes to persuade other countries to provide about 20,000 troops to supplement force in Iraq and Kuwait that currently numbers only 21,000 non-American forces; fresh signal of willingness to share some decision making appears to reflect new flexibility on part of Bush administration, although administration is still insisting that final authority in Iraq remain in American hands (M)

2003-09-05.title: U.S. SAID TO SHIFT APPROACH IN TALKS WITH NORTH KOREA
2003-09-05.abstract: Pres Bush said to shift his approach to North Korea; reportedly authorized American negotiators to say last week in Beijing that he might take steps to help starving nation, by gradually easing sanctions and then perhapssigning peace treaty; American officials say inducements would be phased in slowly only as North Korea starts surrendering its nuclear weapons, dismantling facilities used to develop them and permitting inspectors free run of country; proposals constitute major departure from official White House statements earlier this year that North Korea would see no benefits from new relationship until it shipped all its weapons out of country and dismantled all its nuclear facilities; State Dept official says crucial change in approach at Beijing talks was in providing for sequence of rewards to North Korea; in adopting new strategy, White House apparentely acceded to arguments from within State Dept, and from allies like South Korea, that talks would break down if Washington could not describe some vision of how relations could improve (M)

2003-09-05.title: In U.S. Web, Pakistani Man Contacts Wife
2003-09-05.abstract: Wife of Saifullah Paracha, Pakistani man who disappeared two months ago, says she got letter from her husband saying he is in American detention in Afghanistan; federal prosecutors in Manhattan have charged Paracha's son Uzair with attempting to help associate of Al Qaeda obtain travel documents to enter United States; defense lawyer says son will plead not guilty and may have been unwittingly manipulated by others; says son may have been present at meeting between his father and Khalid Shaikh Mohammed, Al Qaeda's head of operations (M)

2003-09-05.title: Paris Journal; The End of Chocolate (as a Chocolatier Knows It)
2003-09-05.abstract: Christian Constant, noted chocolatier in Paris, France, objects to European Union rule that up to 5 percent of cocoa butter in chocolate may be replaced by other vegetable fats and still be called chocolate; claims pure chocolate consists essentially of cocoa, sugar and cocoa butter; says big chocolate industrialists prefer vegetable fats because they are cheaper; photo (M)

2003-09-05.title: U.S. and Afghan Forces Complete Operation to Root Out Pockets of Taliban
2003-09-05.abstract: Afghan soldiers claim success after 15 days of fighting Taliban in mountains in southeastern part of country; American Special Forces soldiers took part in operation alongside government troops; commander of one Afghan division claims several groups of Taliban fighters have been routed and killed; map (M)

2003-09-05.title: Blix says 'spin' was used to justify the war in Iraq
2003-09-05.abstract: LONDON:—  Hans Blix, the former chief UN weapons inspector, said Thursday that the United States and Britain had employed "spin" in their justification for war in Iraq, issuing one of his strongest criticisms yet of the decision to resort to hostilities.

2003-09-06.title: Protests Thwart Security Rules In Hong Kong
2003-09-06.abstract: Hong Kong chief executive Tung Chee-hwa bows to public pressure and withdraws proposed internal-security laws that spurred huge protests in July; retreat is startling setback for China, which rarely yields to popular demand (M)

2003-09-06.title: Searching for Hussein: G.I.'s, Kurds and Iraqis Are Split on Technique
2003-09-06.abstract: Saddam Hussein, after months of eluding searchers, seems finally in reach; Kurds captured Col Muhammad Rashid Dawdi Aug 19; he led them to former Iraqi Vice Pres Taha Yassin Ramadan and said he met regularly with man in charge of keeping Hussein safe; Kurdish and American officials say everything went wrong after Kurds reluctantly turned Dawdi and Ramadan over to Americans, although they disagree on who failed to keep arrests secret; Kurdish leader Kosrat Rasool says Kurds would have gotten Hussein if Americans had not intervened, interview; photos (M)

2003-09-06.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: President Will Not Run Again
2003-09-06.abstract: 72-year-old German President Johannes Rau announces he will not run for re-election; post is largely ceremonial (S)

2003-09-06.title: World Briefing: Americas
2003-09-06.abstract: BERMUDA: Hurricane Heads Ashore; CANADA: More Fire Evacuations;.

2003-09-06.title: World Briefing | Europe: Spain: Al Jazeera Reporter Arrested
2003-09-06.abstract: Al Jazeera reporter Tayseer Allouni is arrested in Spain and is accused of aiding Al Qaeda's Spanish terrorist cell leader and of funneling money to other operatives in Afghanistan; Al Jazeera director and Allouni's wife say charges are unfounded (S)

2003-09-07.title: Events of Mahmoud Abbas's Tenure
2003-09-07.abstract: Key events of Mahmoud Abbas's tenure as prime minister.

2003-09-07.title: The U.S. Option: Staying the Course
2003-09-07.abstract: News analysis: resignation of Mahmoud Abbas as prime minister of Palestinian Authority has caught Bush administration by surprise, and is dismaying setback for for its 'road map' to peace; official reaction expressing hope that successor could serve as effective partner in peace talks is coded appeal for Palestinians to choose someone who can succeed where Abbas could not in wresting control from Yasir Arafat, quelling violence against Israelies and making it possible for Israel to ease its grip over Gaza and West Bank; but administration has little choice but to watch and hope as Palestinians choose successor; photo (M)

2003-09-07.title: Iranians Divided on Nuclear Inspection Issue
2003-09-07.abstract: Tension among political groups is intensifying over whether Iran will allow more aggressive inspections of its nuclear sites.

2003-09-07.title: Chile's Leader Presses Rights Issues Softly but Successfully
2003-09-07.abstract: Chile is searching for ways to redress human rights abuses of its violent past as it approaches 30th anniversary of military coup that overthrew leftist government of Salvador Allende; plan by Pres Ricardo Lagos's Socialist-led government would force wrongdoers in military to account for their actions in court and would increase efforts to recover and identify bodies of victims from Gen Auguto Pinochet era; plan offers immunity to low-ranking military and civilian officials and reduced sentences to higher-ranking officials in return for testimony to build criminal cases against top brass of military and intelligence apparatus who gave orders in 'disappearances,' killings and torture; some analysts say government is making progress in pursuing rights abusers, but others say it is seeking to overturn amnesty law; photo (M)

2003-09-07.title: Swazi King Encounters Stormy Weather
2003-09-07.abstract: Ferocious thunderstorm with hail and rain descends on Mbabane, Swaziland at climax of annual dance of country's young women before King Mswati III; king frequently uses occasion to select wives; Mswati is coming under increasing criticism for his autocratic rule and unbridled appetites; rural Swazis who regard thunderstorms as omens interpret it as sign from above of dissatisfaction; photo (M)

2003-09-08.title: Cars Seized After Iran's Revolt Find Home and Showroom
2003-09-08.abstract: Modest museum on outskirts of Tehran displays about 40 classic cars seized after Islamic revolution 25 years ago; cars belonged to family of Shah Mohammed Reza Pahlavi and handful of wealthy merchants; museum provides silent testimony to era when power and wealth of Iran's rulers placed them on level of Europe's royal families (M)

2003-09-08.title: Afghan Front Heats Up, and Rumsfeld Urges Patience
2003-09-08.abstract: Lt Gen John R Vines, top American commander in Afghanistan, says his forces killed as many as 200 fighters suspected of being members of Taliban in southern part of country in last two weeks; says infiltration of Taliban has been significant and might include as many as 1,000 fighters in area northeast of Kandahar, previous Taliban stronghold; infiltration adds to American and Afghan concerns about degree to which Pakistan remains sanctuary for Taliban forces; American, Pakistani and Afghan officials are scheduled to meet later this week to discuss matter; Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld holds talks with Pres Hamid Karzai in Kabul; photo (M)

2003-09-08.title: Grim News About Iraq
2003-09-08.abstract: President Bush prepared the electorate for years of occupation, billions more in expense, and many bad days.

2003-09-08.title: In Bush's Words: 'We Will Do What Is Necessary' in the Fight Against Terror
2003-09-08.abstract: Transcript of Pres Bush's address to nation; photo (M)

2003-09-08.title: Prospective Palestinian Premier Says U.S. Must Press Israel
2003-09-08.abstract: Ahmed Qurei, the man who might be Palestinian prime minister, said that for him to succeed in that post the U.S. would have to press for Israeli military restraint.

2003-09-09.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Jordan: 'Honor Killings' Law Blocked
2003-09-09.abstract: Jordan's Parliament, supporting conservative and Islamist views, votes against stricter penalties for men who commit honor killings, in which fathers or brothers kill women who have had sex outside of marriage (S)

2003-09-09.title: North Korea Birthday Party In Japan Illustrates Strains
2003-09-09.abstract: Annual birthday party is held for North Korea at building in Tokyo that serves as North Korea's de facto embassy in Japan, but no Japanese politicians or officials attend this year; anger over North's nuclear bomb program, its missile program and its past practice of kidnapping Japanese has set Japanese public opinion sharply against North Korea and against its supporters in Japan (M)

2003-09-09.title: Man in the News; A Veteran Of Exile Life
2003-09-09.abstract: Man in the News profile of Ahmed Ali Sulieman Qurei, also known as Abu Ala, who has been nominated to serve as Palestinian prime minister; he was born in 1937; photos (M)

2003-09-09.title: Europe Hears Bush's Call For Help Without Scorn
2003-09-09.abstract: In France and Germany, governments that opposed American-led war in Iraq, there is uneasy blend of skepticism and search for something positive in Pres Bush's speech, in which he confessed that he could provide no overall price for reconstruction and no timetable for its completion, or withdrawal of troops; French and German officials react warily to his call on international community to assume its 'responsibility' by committing resources to Iraq (M)

2003-09-09.title: Several Israeli Soldiers Die in Suicide Bombing Outside Base
2003-09-09.abstract: A suicide attacker killed at least six other people today at a bus station in a day of bloodshed throughout the region.

2003-09-10.title: Chile's Wound, 30 Years Later, Is Still Inflamed
2003-09-10.abstract: Many on Chile's left have reached nuanced view of Salvador Allende, leftist president overthrown 30 years ago; many Socialists now argue he erred by failing to drift toward center to woo Christian Democrats; Allende's Communist allies and others on hard left still do not accept view, clinging to his image and programs and thus implying ruling Socialists have abandoned their principles; photos (M)

2003-09-10.title: U.S. aid offer prods Turkey on troops for Iraq
2003-09-10.abstract: WASHINGTON:—  There were hints Tuesday that the U.S. decision to seek a broader United Nations mandate in Iraq might be starting to pay off: The Bush administration said it was ready to provide Turkey with up to $8.5 billion in loans, and the acting Iraqi president said he would welcome up to 10,000 peacekeeping troops from Turkey, under certain conditions.

2003-09-10.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Israeli Rabbi Visits
2003-09-10.abstract: Yona Metzger, Israel's new Ashkenazi chief rabbi, visits large synagogue in center of Moscow and speaks to hundreds of Jews and two dozen rabbis from across Russia; meets with Moscow Mayor Yuri Luzhkov (S)

2003-09-10.title: Militant Behind Bali Blasts Gets Death Sentence
2003-09-10.abstract: A defiant Islamic militant was sentenced to death today after being convicted in the attacks that killed 202 people last year.

2003-09-10.title: Tea and Amnesty Recover Thousands of Antiquities for Iraq
2003-09-10.abstract: More than 3,400 items looted from the national antiquities museum in Baghdad have been recovered, the head of the inquiry said today.

2003-09-11.title: Deal Clears Way for U.N. to Lift Libya Sanctions
2003-09-11.abstract: The Security Council is expected to hold a final vote on Friday, after a vote planned for Tuesday was blocked by the French delegation.

2003-09-11.title: Killed in Iraq
2003-09-11.abstract: Department of Defense confirms death of American in Iraq (S)

2003-09-11.title: A Healer of Terror Victims Becomes One
2003-09-11.abstract: Dr David Applebaum, who spent years helping victims of terrorist attacks in Israel, is himself victim of latest bombing in Jerusalem, along with his daughter; was innovator in emergency medical services during his years of dashing to bomb sites; had just returned to Israel from New York conference dealing with terror attacks, during which he gave step-by-step presentation on how major bombings are handled at hospital in Israel; was in cafe in Jerusalem with his daughter Nava to discuss her upcoming marriage at time of suicide bombing; photos (M)

2003-09-11.title: Swedish Foreign Minister Wounded in Knife Attack While Shopping
2003-09-11.abstract: Unidentified assailant stabs Swedish Foreign Min Anna Lindh as she shops in store in Stockholm; she is in critical condition after surgery; attack stuns many Swedes and rekindles memories of killing of Prime Min Olof Palme in 1986; an emotional Prime Min Goran Persson says attack on Lindh is attack on Sweden's open society; Lindh has been leading voice in favor of adopting euro, which Sweden will vote on Sept 14 (M)

2003-09-11.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-09-11.abstract: SERBIA: Former Foes Exchange Apologies; ITALY: Judges Plan Short Strike Over Insult; BRITAIN: Russian Tycoon Receives Asylum; IT JUST RAINED A LOT; FRANCE: Need A Nose Cone?

2003-09-12.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-09-12.abstract: BARENTS SEA: No Radioactivity At Sub; LIECHTENSTEIN: Monitoring Democracy;.

2003-09-12.title: A Mean Scrum on Playing Fields of South Africa
2003-09-12.abstract: White South African rugby player's refusal to share room with black player recalls specter of apartheid; although rugby has been popular among South African blacks for decades, its most zealous fans are Afrikaners, who became synonymous with apartheid and its cruelties; fact that white player Geo Cronje comes from northern stronghold of lingering apartheid sympathy has made feud into national incident; photos of Cronje and of Quinton Davids, black player he snubbed (M)

2003-09-12.title: On Trip, Pope Displays Fluctuating Levels of Vigor
2003-09-12.abstract: On the 102nd foreign trip of his papacy, Pope John Paul II has projected a confusing portrait of resilience and weakness.

2003-09-12.title: Bush Tells Returning Troops Iraq Is Crucible in Fight on Terror
2003-09-12.abstract: President Bush paid tribute today to some of the American soldiers who swept Saddam Hussein from power.

2003-09-12.title: Iranian Envoy Blames U.S. for Nation's Reticence on Nuclear Plans
2003-09-12.abstract: Iran's permanent representative to United Nations Javad Zarif blames Americans for his country's reluctance to reveal details of its nuclear program; claims Washington's intent to deprive Iran of legitimate power plants is reason for Iran's current problems with International Atomic Energy Agency; IAEA is expected to approve resolution to give Iran until Oct 31 to answer agency's questions about its nuclear program; United States supports resolution but is not its sponsor (M)

2003-09-13.title: Israel Rejects Wide Criticism of Its Threat to Exile Arafat
2003-09-13.abstract: Israel fends off worldwide criticism of its decision in principle to oust Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat, even though it seems unlikely for now to carry out threat; Israeli analysts see threat intended at least in part to address public anger at two suicide bombings; Prime Min Ariel Sharon has strong public support in Israel for removing him; photos (M)0

2003-09-13.title: Iraqi Shiite Calls Police Policy Unfair
2003-09-13.abstract: The leader of the main Shiite political group lashed out at the occupation authorities for failing to integrate former Shiite militia members into the police force.

2003-09-13.title: World Briefing | Europe: Spain: Judge Reopens Qaeda Case
2003-09-13.abstract: Spanish high court Judge Guillermo Ruiz Polanco reopens case against four men suspected of working with Al Qaeda; they were arrested and then released last January; although chemicals found in their possession when arrested were laundry and household products, authorities have determined that when combined chemicals could produce version of napalm (S)

2003-09-13.title: Peace and Reason Amid Chaos, a Balm for Pain
2003-09-13.abstract: Profile of Roman Catholic Abp Michael Kpakala Francis of Liberia, who spoke out against nation's ousted warlord president, Charles G Taylor, despite constant death threats; Amb John W Blaney credits him with keeping hope alive; photo (M)

2003-09-13.title: Swedish Police May Have Video of Foreign Minister's Killer
2003-09-13.abstract: Thousands of Swedes continue to pay tribute to Min Anna Lindh, who was fatally stabbed at Stockholm department store; clog central square, Stockholm, for rally against violence; police say they may have videotape of her killer; poll results are mixed on impact of her death on referendum she backed on joining euro, currency used by 12 European Union members; photo (M)

2003-09-14.title: Burmese Defy U.S. Demands For Release Of Activist
2003-09-14.abstract: Burmese military junta is not expected to release pro-democracy leader Daw Aung San Suu Kyi any time soon; photo (S)

2003-09-14.title: Powell's Remarks
2003-09-14.abstract: Following is an excerpt of a news conference held by Secretary of State Colin L. Powell and L. Paul Bremer III, the chief American administrator for Iraq, on Sunday in Baghdad, as transcribed by Federal Document Clearing House Inc.

2003-09-14.title: On Sunday Talk Shows, U.S. Officials Defend Iraq Policy
2003-09-14.abstract: The Bush administration sought to deflect charges that it was too optimistic in planning troop levels and money needed for Iraq.

2003-09-14.title: Powell Says Progress Is Being Made and Urges Patience
2003-09-14.abstract: The secretary of state warned that the process of restoring sovereignty to Iraq might not be seen as legitimate if it goes too rapidly.

2003-09-14.title: Zimbabwe Police Close Down Nation's Largest Daily Paper
2003-09-14.abstract: Zimbabwe police shut down major newspaper, Daily News, after court rules paper has been publishing illegally; action silences one of few independent news outlets (S)

2003-09-15.title: Powell's Remarks: 'Not Occupiers'
2003-09-15.abstract: Excerpts from remarks by Secretary of State Colin L Powell in Baghdad (S)

2003-09-15.title: The Fourth Wife
2003-09-15.abstract: The deposed dictator's turbulent family life: Only last year Saddam Hussein married a woman of German origin.

2003-09-15.title: POORER COUNTRIES PULL OUT OF TALKS OVER WORLD TRADE
2003-09-15.abstract: World trade talks intended to help developing nations collapse when delegates from many poorer nations walk out, accusing wealthy nations of failing to offer sufficient compromises on agriculture and other issues; World Trade Orgn will now almost certainly fail to make its self-imposed deadline of Jan 2005 for reaching new agreement that dismantles global trade barriers; failure also means faltering global economy will not receive jump-start by expansion of markets; wealthy nations had hoped agreement would help fend off new wave of protectionism, especially in United States, where manufacturing jobs have been disappearing by tens of thousands, and where questions about benefits of unfettered world trade have already infected presidential campaign; pivotal issue in breakdown of talks is agriculture, and developing nations' objection to $300 billion in subsidies paid to world's wealthiest farmers; proposals made by US and Europe to redress what developing nations regard as major injustice fall far short of their expectations; photo (M)

2003-09-15.title: Bush Aides on TV to Defend Iraq Policy
2003-09-15.abstract: Senior civilian and military officials in Bush adminstration blanket Sunday morning talk programs to defend its Iraq policy, in particular to deflect charges that it was overly optimistic in planning troop levels and money required to win peace; Vice Pres Dick Cheney concedes that he misspoke when, during television interview last spring, he said Iraq had 'reconstituted nuclear weapons'; but he expresses confidence that evidence that Hussein had program for unconventional weapons will be uncovered; photo (M)

2003-09-15.title: Powell, in Iraq, Visits Memorial to Kurds Gassed in '88
2003-09-15.abstract: If the secretary of state's visit underscored an unwelcome fact, it was that evidence of Iraq's banned arms has yet to be found.

2003-09-16.title: World Briefing | Asia: Afghanistan: 15 Taliban Killed, U.S. Says
2003-09-16.abstract: US forces at Bagram Air Base, Kabul, Afghanistan, report Operation Mountain Viper airstrikes have killed 15 suspected Taliban as part of effort to quell insurgency in south and southeast Afghanistan (S)

2003-09-16.title: Soldiers Search For 8 Taken In Colombia
2003-09-16.abstract: Hundreds of Colombian troops swarm over coastal mountain range in northern Colombia searching for eight foreign tourists who were kidnapped Sept 12; operation is closely monitored by Pres Alvaro Uribe; rebels generally hold foreigners for ransom; also being held are three American Defense Dept contractors captured in Feb, 23 Colombian politicians and 45 soldiers and policemen (M)

2003-09-16.title: Killing Arafat as Official Policy Is Denied
2003-09-16.abstract: Israeli Foreign Min Silvan Shalom says killing Yasir Arafat is 'not the official policy' of his government, one day after Israel's Vice Prime Minister Ehud Olmert said it was one option under consideration (S)

2003-09-16.title: World Briefing: Americas
2003-09-16.abstract: ARGENTINA: Mayor Re-Elected; CANADA: Drug Users' Center Opens;.

2003-09-16.title: U.S. Says 8 Captives in Iraq Claim to Be American or British
2003-09-16.abstract: The detainees are suspected of carrying out or planning attacks on American troops or allied forces, a U.S. general said.

2003-09-17.title: 6 Held in Iraq By U.S. Claim To Be American
2003-09-17.abstract: Brig Gen Janis Karpinski says six people identifying selves as Americans and two saying they are British are among 4,400 'security detainees' being held in connection with guerrilla attacks on allied forces in Iraq; says classification means they have fewer rights than prisoners of war; makes remark during tour of Abu Ghraib prison, outside Baghdad, where they are among some 10,000 prisoners; Sec Donald Rumsfeld and other officials say there is little certainty about men's identities, nationalities or even what they were doing in Iraq; military reports scattered attacks on occupying forces; Khaldiya's small and beleaguered police department vows to stay on duty despite slaying of new chief Col Khudheir Mikhlif Ali; photos (M)

2003-09-17.title: 'Hussein' Tape Calls U.S. Withdrawal From Iraq Inevitable
2003-09-17.abstract: The broadcast came as the U.S. military cast further doubt on the possibility that six prisoners might be American, as they claimed.

2003-09-17.title: U.S. Cutting Loan Guarantees To Oppose Israeli Settlements
2003-09-17.abstract: Bush administration, in almost totally symbolic move, cuts loan guarantees to Israel to meet Congressional curbs on financial support to Israeli's continued expansion of settlements in West Bank and Gaza; says it might further limit guarantees to protest Israel's construction of barrier dividing what it considers its land from Palestinian territory; move comes as Israel sells $1.6 billion of bonds on Wall Street, all backed by United States guarantees; is rare but widely expected slap at Israeli policy as US struggles to keep its peace proposal alive; national security adviser Condoleezza Rice says US opposes any move by Israel to exile or kill Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat, but that international community must decide how to deal with him as obstacle to peace, interview; says he systematically undermined Mahmoud Abbas, who resigned as Palestinian prime minister (M)

2003-09-17.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-09-17.abstract: JAPAN: Living Long, 2 Days At A Time; JAPAN: Explosion Ends Hostage Siege; SINGAPORE: Sars Patient Released; NEPAL: Rebels Charged With Murder;.

2003-09-17.title: Women Called Mideast's Untapped Resource
2003-09-17.abstract: World Bank says women remain 'huge, untapped' economic resource in Mideast and North Africa, where more women workers are needed to transform economies that must defend increasingly on private-sector exports to compete worldwide; says women comprise only 32 percent of labor force in those regions and face greater obstacles in finding jobs than female contemporaries elsewhere (M)

2003-09-18.title: Doubt on Prisoners' Claims
2003-09-18.abstract: As a voice professing to be Saddam Hussein's urged more attacks on Americans, the military challenged claims by six suspects in such attacks that they are Americans. Page A13.

2003-09-18.title: Spanish Judge Is Charging Bin Laden and 9 in 9/11 Plot
2003-09-18.abstract: Judge Baltasar Garzon of Spain charges Osama bin Laden and nine people identified as members of his Al Qaeda network with committing Sept 11, 2001, attacks and other acts of terrorism; says his international detention order is needed to help ensure they are brought to justice when they are located; he further justifies his order on grounds that terrorists acts were 'planned partially in Spain'; Garzon has history of pursuing cases beyond Spain's boundaries, thanks to Spanish constitutional provision affording 'universal jurisdiction' in prosecuting crimes against humanity (M)

2003-09-18.title: Afghanistan And Iraq Tab Of $87 Billion Is Submitted
2003-09-18.abstract: President Bush formally asks Congress for $87 billion to rebuild Iraq and Afghanistan and support troops there; three-fourths of money would be used to pay for military occupation of Iraq; request also includes $5.7 billion to rebuild Iraq's electrical network, $3.7 billion for improved water and sewer system and $5.1 billion for new civil security system; $850 million is requested to build new hospital in Iraq along with other health improvements; Sen George V Voinovich, fiscally conservative Republican, says Americans will wonder about fairness of such vast public works programs in Iraq but not at home (M)

2003-09-18.title: Israeli Troops Kill Hamas Militant in Big Sweep Into Gaza
2003-09-18.abstract: The Israeli Army today mounted its largest incursion into the Gaza Strip since its partial withdrawal in June.

2003-09-18.title: Russia's Chechen Plan: Pick a Leader and Leave
2003-09-18.abstract: Akhmad Kadyrov, Kremlin's choice in next month's presidential election in Chechnya, is widely expected to win; his two most dangerous challengers were forced by government to withdraw; many analysts say election is being stage-managed by Moscow as part of exit strategy from unending, unwinnable, horribly destructive war; say that after four years of slaughter, Russian people want their government to just declare victory and leave; photo; map (M)

2003-09-19.title: U.N. Senses It Must Change, Fast, or Fade Away
2003-09-19.abstract: Secretary Gen Kofi Annan is increasingly insistent that member states end decade of dawdling and remake United Nations to fit geopolitical realities of 21st century; claims only 'radical' revisions in institution are likely to preserve it; will outline plans for reform at annual General Assembly gathering next week; wants Security Council to be enlarged, and General Assembly remade so it does not simply pass 'lowest common denominator' nostrums; other UN leaders worry whether any change can give UN freedom it needs to survive without being seen as either lackey of United States or easily swattable gadfly; photos (M)

2003-09-19.title: World Briefing | The Americas: Brazil: Making Anti-Semitism A Crime
2003-09-19.abstract: Brazil's Supreme Court, addressing state charges that published works of Nazi sympathizer Siegfried Ellwanger are hostile against Jews, rules anti-semitism is type of racism and therefore punishable by law (S)

2003-09-19.title: The Collections / New York : Kors puts fashion back on the sunny side of the street
2003-09-19.abstract: As Britney Spears, pop angel turned temptress, hiked her black and white sweater dress above thigh-high boots, her appearance at the Diesel show summed up the mood of New York's summer 2004 season.

2003-09-19.title: Bush Admits Mideast Plan Is Stalled and Blames Arafat
2003-09-19.abstract: Pres Bush acknowledges for first time that Middle East peace talks he sponsored have stalled; lays blame solely on Yasir Arafat; says Palestinian Prime Min Mahoud Abbas, now resigned, was undermined at all turns by Arafat; makes remarks at joint news conference with King Abdullah of Jordan at Camp David; Bush offers no clear path for how he will revive peace talks, nor does he mention Ahmed Qurei, Arafat's nominee to replace Abbas as prime minister; simply repeats his demand that Palestinian terrorism must end; photo (M)

2003-09-19.title: Israeli Troops Hit Gaza; Palestinian Forces Battle Hamas
2003-09-19.abstract: Israeli Army stages its largest raid into Gaza Strip since its partial withdrawal in June, sweeping into central refugee camp and killing Hamas militant in firefight; later, Palestinian security forces clash with Hamas gunmen in dispute that demonstrates turbulence in Palestinian politics; senior Palestinian official says Hamas members burned down police station and threw grenades; Palestinian Interior Ministry issues statement saying Hamas kidnapped security official and that security forces then arrested seven Hamas men to interrogate them; Muhammad Dahlan, who controls Interior Ministry, was appointed by former Prime Min Mahmoud Abbas; Hamas denies starting fight to test Dahlan's resolve; photo (M)

2003-09-20.title: Clinton, on a Visit to Kosovo, Warns Against Getting Even
2003-09-20.abstract: Ex-Pres Clinton, arriving in Pristina, Kosovo, to get honorary degree and visit with American soldiers in United Nations peace-keeping force, is greeted with acclaim by hundreds of people lining roadside; is seen by province's ethnic Albanian majority as responsible for ending Yugoslav rule and taking province step closer to independence; warns Albanians that seeking revenge for atrocities by Serbian and Yugoslavian forces could hinder independence effort, speech, Pristina University; holds American troops should remain in Balkans until job is finished; photo (M)

2003-09-20.title: World Briefing | United Nations: Force For Liberia Approved
2003-09-20.abstract: United Nations Security Council approves deployment of up to 15,000 troops to keep peace and disarm rebels in Liberia; United Nations Mission in Liberia will take control of region on October 1 and relieve West African Forces currently in place (S)

2003-09-20.title: World Briefing | Africa: Zimbabwe: Police Keep Paper From Publishing
2003-09-20.abstract: Samuel Sipepa Nkomo, Daily News chief executive, announces Zimbabwe police have ignored court ruling to allow newspaper to reopen; staff of newspaper, known to oppose President Robert Mugabe, is sent home by police (S)

2003-09-20.title: French Minister Threatens To Expel Extremist Muslims
2003-09-20.abstract: French Interior Min Nicolas Sarkozy threatens to close any mosque considered extremist and expel any Muslim prayer leader who preaches radical message, newspaper interview; pledges to deny visas to Muslim participants in conferences who do not respect French values; has sought to set strict limits on behavior of nation's growing Muslim community; seeks to create official Islam for France and show that most Muslims are mainstream, law-abiding citizens (M)

2003-09-20.title: U.S. Is Struggling to Make New Iraq Resolution Matter
2003-09-20.abstract: United States is struggling at United Nations for new resolution that will change military and political realities in Iraq, not merely win Security Council passage; wants to spur contributions of troops and money; American diplomats say draft US proposes moves toward meeting other Council members' desire for clear timetable for transfer of power from occupation authority to Iraqis; it is unclear whether US is willing to surrender control of Iraq's political transformation to UN or to Iraqi provisional government; table of each nation's troop contingent in Iraq (M)

2003-09-21.title: At Memorial In Bosnia, Clinton Helps Mourn 7,000
2003-09-21.abstract: Former Pres Bill Clinton attends official opening of museum and cemetery near Srebenica, Bosnia, to honor 7,000 Muslim men who were massacred by Serbs soldiers there in 1995; many of women and politicians attending ceremony acknowledge that Clinton was instrumental in stopping Bosnian war, but some say he waited too long; many Serbs in Bosnia still do not acknowledge massacre; photo (M)

2003-09-21.title: 3 Leaders' Views on Iraq, Not Quite Unified
2003-09-21.abstract: Chancellor Gerhard Schroder of Germany, Pres Jacques Chiraq of France and Prime Min Tony Blair of Britain meet in Berlin in effort to reach common position on American draft of Security Council resolution authorizing countries to contribute money and troops in rebuilding of Iraq, but they appear to make no progress; agree that sovereignty should be transferred to Iraqi civilian authority as soon as possible and that UN should have key role in that process; Pres Vladimir Putin of Russia echoes their views; photo (M)

2003-09-21.title: Chirac Calls for Transfer of Sovereignty to Iraqi People
2003-09-21.abstract: In an hourlong interview, President Jacques Chirac laid out for the first time a two-stage plan for Iraqi self-rule.

2003-09-21.title: Divorce in South Korea: Striking a New Attitude
2003-09-21.abstract: Divorce rate is soaring in South Korea, reflecting social transformation that is shaking country still anchored in Confucian values of family and patriarchy; majority of divorces are initiated by women, who are more educated and more likely to be employed than they were in past and who are no longer placed at disadvantage by divorce laws; personality conflict is reason most often cited for seeking divorce; Love and War, reality television show about troubled marriages and divorce, consistently ranks high in ratings; divorce rate graph; photos (M)

2003-09-21.title: Housing Plan For Top Aides In Afghanistan Draws Rebuke
2003-09-21.abstract: Outrage over plan to build high-end residence in central Kabul for government officials threatens to discredit Pres Hamid Karzai's administration, which has already been losing support because of perceived injustices and government's slowness in its reconstruction efforts; Karzai denounces plans and orders inquiry and freeze on building in neighborhood (M)

2003-09-22.title: Breakup of Ice Shelf Signals Climate Change, Scientists Say
2003-09-22.abstract: An ice shelf that has jutted into the Arctic Ocean from Canada for at least 3,000 years has broken up over the last two years.

2003-09-22.title: Full Transcript of Interview With Jacques Chirac
2003-09-22.abstract: Following is a transcript of an interview with President Jacques Chirac of France. The interview was conducted in French and translated by The New York Times.

2003-09-22.title: Bavaria's Conservatives Show Strength in German State Election
2003-09-22.abstract: Bavaria's conservative party swaggers to victory in state parliamentary election, extending its four-decade reign over Germany's most economically vibrant region and dealing worse-than-expected defeat to Chancellor Gerhard Schroder's Social Democratic Party; lopsidedness of victory leads analysts to assume that Bavarians think little of Schroder's handling of German economy; photo (M)

2003-09-22.title: French Leader Outlines Two-Stage Proposal
2003-09-22.abstract: Pres Jacques Chirac says France will approve new United Nations resolution only if it recognizes need for immediate transfer of sovereignty in Iraq to Iraqis, int; lays out two-stage plan for Iraqi self-rule, with first stage being symbolic transfer of sovereignty from American hands to existing 25-member Iraqi Governing Council, followed by gradual ceding of real power over period of six to nine months; says France is ready to train Iraqi police officers and soldiers--either in or out of Iraq--if Security Council could agree on empowering Iraqis at once; says France has no intention of sending troops to be part of American-led occupation force, but suggests that circumstances could change; says he does not intend to veto Bush administration's proposed United Nations resolution to attract more foreign troops and international funds to Iraq unless it becomes 'provocative'; but says France will abstain unless resolution includes deadline for transfer of sovereignty and timetable for transfer of power, as well as 'key role' for UN; photos (M)

2003-09-22.title: Bomb Explodes Outside U.N. Mission in Iraq, Killing Officer
2003-09-22.abstract: The bombing came a month after 23 people died, including the U.N. envoy to Iraq, in a suicide attack on the U.N. compound.

2003-09-23.title: IRAQ COUNCIL HEAD SHIFTS TO POSITION AT ODDS WITH U.S.
2003-09-23.abstract: Ahmad Chalabi, president of Iraq's interim government, is in New York to press alternatives to Bush administration's occupation policy in postwar Iraq, and perhaps also to complete his personal transformation from protege of Pentagon conservatives to Iraqi nationalist with loud, independent voice; professes gratitude to Bush administration for toppling Saddam Hussein's government but, contrary to US plan, he is demanding that Iraqi Governing Council be given at least partial control of powerful finance and security ministries, and rejects idea of more foreign troops coming to Iraq; says his strategy is to get from UN General Assembly sovereign status for unelected 25-member Governing Council; says he has sent representatives to France and Germany to discuss putting Iraqis back in charge under new UN mandate that would end American control of occupation, even if Aemrican troops remain in Iraq; denies being at cross purposes with Americans, but his arrival in New York with delegation determined to advance clock on sovereignty puts him and interim government he heads in direct confrontation with Pres Bush; photo (M)

2003-09-23.title: In Chiracs Words: Call a Summit
2003-09-23.abstract: The following is the text of President Jacques Chirac's remarks to the General Assembly as recorded by Federal News Service, Inc.

2003-09-23.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-09-23.abstract: GERMANY: Memorial Rejected; RUSSIA: A Base Abroad; FINLAND: Minks Hunted After Fur Farm Raid;.

2003-09-23.title: In Annans Words: Fork in the Road
2003-09-23.abstract: UNITED NATIONS, Sept. 23 — Following is transcript from a speech today at the General Assembly by Secretary General Kofi Annan as supplied by the United Nations.

2003-09-23.title: Huge Ice Shelf Is Reported To Break Up In Canada
2003-09-23.abstract: Large ice shelf that jutted into Arctic Ocean from northernmost Canada for at least 3,000 years has broken up over last two years; scientists say this provides fresh evidence that region is warming past thresholds that can produce abrupt changes; photo; maps (M)

2003-09-24.title: Bush, at U.N., Defends Policy Over Iraq
2003-09-24.abstract: President Bush was vigorous in his defense of the war even as he sought the financial assistance of a still cautious world in rebuilding Iraq.

2003-09-24.title: Partisan Battle Builds in Congress Over Bush's Spending Request
2003-09-24.abstract: Huge sums requested by White House to occupy and rebuild Iraq sharply inflame partisan tensions in Congress, with Republican leaders accusing Democrats of undermining military and sowing division by criticizing Bush administration's postwar policies; many Republicans share unease over size of request, but say they have to support Bush when polls show his approval levels declining; survey by Democracy Corps, firm run by former members of Clinton administrtaion, finds that 60 percent of public opposes $87 million request and that 54 percent believes Bush does not have plan to win peace and bring troops home (M)

2003-09-24.title: World Briefing | Africa: Kenya: 30 Million Guns
2003-09-24.abstract: Peter Batchelor, Small Arms Survey independent research group director, reports there are estimated 30 million small arms in sub-Saharan Africa, not 100 million generally believed; number suggests conventional methods of gun control can succeed in reducing unnecessary firearms (S)

2003-09-24.title: In a Poll, Baghdad Residents Call Freedom Worth the Price
2003-09-24.abstract: Gallup poll of Baghdad residents finds that nearly two-thirds believe removal of Pres Saddam Hussein has been worth hardship they have been forced to endure; only 8 percent of respondents say they believe that their lives are worse off as result of military campaign to remove Hussein and his Baath Party leadership; respondents still suspect motives of US and Britain and hold Pres Jacques Chrirac of France in higher regard than Pres Bush and Prime Min Tony Blair; are nearly equally divided on whether American-British occupation authority, led by L Paul Bremer III, is doing good job running country, but half say occupation authority is doing better job now than it was two months ago (M)

2003-09-24.title: In Bush's Words: 'Advance of Democratic Institutions in Iraq Is Setting an Example'
2003-09-24.abstract: Text of Pres Bush's speech to United Nations General Assembly

2003-09-25.title: Reykjavik Journal; Drop That Harpoon! Whale Hostilities Revisited
2003-09-25.abstract: After 14-year hiatus, Icelandic whale hunters bag their first whale as part of new scientific research program that falls outside 1986 ban on commercial whaling; photos (M)

2003-09-25.title: New fashion statement:'Save Africa' : British designer agitates for AIDs awareness
2003-09-25.abstract: LONDON:—  The words were spelled out in rhinestones across the chest of one of the world's sexiest models: "Use a condom" read Naomi Campbell's tank top.

2003-09-25.title: U.S. Remains Leader in Global Arms Sales, Report Says
2003-09-25.abstract: New Congressional report finds United States maintained its dominance in international arms market last year, especially in sales to developing nations; total worldwide sales for US in 2002 was $13.3 billion, or 45.5 percent of global conventional weapons deals; Russia is second in sales to developing world, with $5 billion, followed by France with $1 billion (M)

2003-09-25.title: Democrats Step Up Attacks on Iraq War
2003-09-25.abstract: Congressional Democrats press forward with bitter criticism of Bush administration's handling of aftermath of Iraq war; this is their first direct challenge of White House after months of treading softly when it comes to Iraqi conflict; Democrats sense opening against Pres Bush, and they are finding fault with everything from administration's rationale for war and lack of postwar planning to its diplomatic efforts and even Bush's decision to leave UN before others finished speaking; chart beaking down billions of dollars that administration is requesting for reconstruction of Iraq (M)

2003-09-26.title: Charity Said to Have Paid Terrorists Is Under Investigation by the Saudis
2003-09-26.abstract: Al Haramain Charitable Foundation, Saudi charity which has been accused by Western officials of sponsoring terrorism, is now subject of criminal investigation by Saudi Arabia, as is founder Ageel al-Ageel; American intelligence recently gave Saudi officials detailed document outlining terrorism links with International Islamic Relief Orgn, another major Saudi charity (M)

2003-09-26.title: Inspectors in Iran Find Highly Enriched Uranium at an Electrical Plant
2003-09-26.abstract: Inspectors for International Atomic Energy Agency find traces of highly enriched uranium at electrical plant on outskirts of Tehran, second site in Iran where such evidence of unreported enrichment activities has been discovered in recent months; latest finding further ratchets up pressure on Iran to prove that its nuclear program is intended solely for peaceful purposes (M)

2003-09-26.title: Q&A / Peter Medgyessy : 'This European entity is a cohesive force'
2003-09-26.abstract: Prime Minister Peter Medgyessy, an economist by training and a former finance minister, has led Hungary's center-left coalition since May 2002. He recently spoke to Thomas Fuller of the International Herald Tribune. Medgyessy's answers were interpreted from Hungarian into English.

2003-09-26.title: Aquino's daughter accuses boyfriend of abuse
2003-09-26.abstract: MANILA:—  In a riveting display of rage and self-flagellation, a popular television host and daughter of a martyred politician has appeared on national television alleging that her womanizing lover beat her up, aimed a gun at her during a fight and infected her with a sexually transmitted disease.

2003-09-26.title: In Reversal, Brazil Will Permit a Gene-Modified Crop
2003-09-26.abstract: Brazils government has abandoned a pledge and decided to allow farmers to grow genetically modified soybeans.

2003-09-27.title: Gunman Kills Two as Family Celebrates Jewish New Year
2003-09-27.abstract: Palestinian gunman opens fire on Israeli family celebrating Rosh Hashana in settlement near Hebron, killing man and baby girl and wounding infant's parents before being killed by soldiers; Prime Min Ariel Sharon, in interview, discusses difficulty of seizing Yasir Arafat without harming him and says US objections will be taken into account; Israeli troops arrest 25 Hamas suspects in Ramallah sweep (M)

2003-09-27.title: Killed in Iraq
2003-09-27.abstract: List of Americans newly confirmed dead in Iraq; 307 members of US military have died there since start of war

2003-09-27.title: Son's Wish to Die, And Mother's Help, Stir French Debate
2003-09-27.abstract: French debate emotional issue of assisted suicide following death of Vincent Humbert, 22-year-old paraplegic who wrote book asking for right to die and passed away after mother administered overdose of sedatives; Marie Humbert acted on third anniversary of car accident that left her son paralyzed, mute and blind; photo; she is taken in custody and then released and hospitalized, having won enormous public sympathy for her campaign to help her son and legalize euthanasia (M)

2003-09-27.title: U.S., Russia And Europe Meet at U.N. On Mideast
2003-09-27.abstract: US, European Union and Russian senior envoys meet at UN on deteriorating situation in Mideast, renewing appeals to Palestinians to stop suicide bombings and to Israel to exercise restraint in responses; European Union, which finances about one-third of Palestinian Authority, will press Yasir Arafat to allow prime minister Ahmed Qurei to crack down on terrorists; Prime Min Ariel Sharon says US objections to expelling Arafat will be taken into account; Sec Colin Powell reportedly says US will press Israel to free more Palestinian prisoners, ease security measures, dismantle some settlements and stop construction of barrier; Sec Gen Kofi Annan bluntly acknowledges dead end and calls for bold steps (M)

2003-09-27.title: With Issues to Resolve, Bush Welcomes Putin to Camp David
2003-09-27.abstract: Pres Bush welcomes Pres Vladimir Putin to Camp David to demonstrate that two are problem-solvers working together, despite divisions on Iraq and Russia's sale of nuclear technology to Iran (S)

2003-09-28.title: A Swirl of Foreboding In Mahogany's Grain
2003-09-28.abstract: Article examines illegal mahogany trade in Peru, world's largest producer; timber, much of which is logged illegally by small traders deep in rain forest, moves through chain of corrupt officials and exporters to lumber yards, which are uncertain of its origin; Peru is taking new steps to combat illegal trade, having passed modern forest management law, and is also working to meet United Nations deadline to ensure all mahogany has been extracted legally; illustration shows areas that have been plundered by loggers and markup of lumber as it passes though market; photo (L)

2003-09-28.title: As Tobacco Warnings Loom, Scornful Spaniards Light Up
2003-09-28.abstract: New health warnings, which must cover about one-third of the surface area of every cigarette package in the country, are not stopping people from smoking.

2003-09-28.title: U.S. Compound in Baghdad Is Hit in Attack
2003-09-28.abstract: Rashid Hotel, heavily protected compound for Americans in downtown Baghdad, is hit by three projectiles; they cause little damage and no injuries, but after several weeks of high-profile attacks and beefed-up security around Baghdad, strike is seen as message that Americans will be target no matter how much they seek to protect themselves; in Falluja, where relations between American troops and residents have been bad from start, American military reports killing two Iraqis who fired at them as they tried to run checkpoint; incident raises possibility of further revenge attacks against troops there; photo (M)

2003-09-28.title: On Alert for Al Qaeda in Iraq, U.S. Is Tracking 2 Suspects
2003-09-28.abstract: US investigators and Special Forces in Iraq are pursuing Abdul Rahman Yasin and Abu Musab al-Zarqawi, known to have had prior connections to Osama bin Laden; Yasin was indicted in connection with 1993 World Trade Center bombing and is on FBI's most wanted terrorist list; he has been living in Iraq for about 10 years; US says al-Zarqawi has been bringing money into Iraq and trying to establish beachhead there; Coalition Provisional Authority head L Paul Bremer says at least 19 Qaeda members are in custody but offers few details; alliances between Iraqis and fundamentalists are considered uneasy, as Iraqis are more secular; estimates of number of Qaeda fighters in Iraq vary from several hundred to as many as 2,000 (M)

2003-09-28.title: Fighting in Kashmir Revives Rancor in Pakistan and India
2003-09-28.abstract: Peace effort between India and Pakistan is jeopardized by surging violence in disputed territory of Kashmir, where more than 300 people have been killed in last month; India accuses Pakistan of covertly backing insurgency against Indian-held part of Kashmir; Pakistan denies charge and calls insurgency indigenous freedom struggle; rancor is taking toll on peace effort; map; photo (M)

2003-09-29.title: Pope Names 31 Cardinals, Future Voters on Succession
2003-09-29.abstract: Pope John Paul II announces selection of 31 new cardinals, move that significantly deepens his already profound influence over elite group of Roman Catholic leaders who will choose his successor; announcement comes months earlier than many Vatican officials had expected; some suspect that pope and his closest aides are increasingly worried about his health and want him to seize at least one more chance to mold future of church with estimated one billion followers worldwide; photo; chart (M)

2003-09-29.title: Chinas Ties With Japan Strained by Racy Report
2003-09-29.abstract: Japanese tourists are reported to have staged a public display of promiscuity on a sensitive anniversary in Sino-Japanese relations.

2003-09-29.title: New Criticism On Prewar Use Of Intelligence
2003-09-29.abstract: Bush administration faces renewed criticism about how it managed intelligence before war in Iraq, and internal tensions over leak of CIA agent's identity; leaders of House Intelligence Committee reopen debate over rationale for war; their critical interim assessment states that intelligence agencies relied on outdated and inadequate information to conclude that Iraq had forbidden weapons and ties to Al Qaeda; at same time, officials confirm that CIA Dir George J Tenet has asked Justice Dept to look into whether one or more administration officials leaked information to news media disclosing identity of covert CIA agent; agent is wife of Joseph C Wilson 4th, former ambassador to Gabon who, more than 18 months ago, concluded in report to CIA that there is no evidence that Saddam Hussein tried to buy uranium ore in Niger in effort to build nuclear arms; his report was ignored and he has been highly critical of how administration handled intelligence claims about Iraq's nuclear weapons programs, suggesting that aides to Pres Bush and Vice Pres Cheney tried to inflate threat; very fact that Tenet referred matter to Justice Dept comes as major political embarrassment to White House that is famously tight-lipped, and to president who has repeatedly vowed that his administration would never leak classified information (M)

2003-09-29.title: Stunted by Illness, Tibetan Villagers Ponder Flight
2003-09-29.abstract: Roughly 9 percent of population of Tibet has Kashen-Beck disease, also called Big Bone disease; in severe cases, disease can cause long bones in arms and legs to stop growing during childhood; result is adults who stand barely four feet tall; knotted wrists and swollen elbows are also part of disease; scientists have yet to discover cure, but they believe bad water, poor diet and crops grown in mineral-deficient soil are at least partly to blame; photos (M)

2003-09-29.title: Most of Italy Is Blacked Out for Several Hours
2003-09-29.abstract: Power failure leaves most of Italy without electricity for several hours, interrupting rail and air traffic, jamming emergency phone lines and forcing thousands of Romans into makeshift refuges in subway stations; at least three people die as direct result of blackout; Industry Min Antonio Marzano says Italian grid is not to blame for power failure; says investigation into cause will begin immediately; France and Switzerland have confirmed that their power lines were disabled, but refuse to accept responsibility for blackout and dismiss chain reaction theory (M)

2003-09-30.title: Palestinian Ends Defense in Murder Trial
2003-09-30.abstract: Marwan Barghouti, top leader of Palestinian uprising, is on trial for orchestrating killing of 26 Israelis; suggests that Israeli occupation of Gaza and West Bank could lead to Israel's being submerged in 'one state for two peoples,' with Jews and Palestinians living under common rule; his yearlong trial has become focal point for Israelis and Palestinians; photo (M)

2003-09-30.title: Drafting of Charter Deeply Divides Iraq
2003-09-30.abstract: As the Iraqi Governing Council presses for a transfer of sovereignty to Iraqis, a dispute over who will control the drafting of a constitution is embroiling Shiites, Sunnis and Kurds.    Iraqi officials who have been deliberating for two months as a 25-member committee to recommend a procedure for drafting the constitution said they were deadlocked.

2003-09-30.title: World Briefing | Asia: China: North Korea Refugees Secretly Ousted
2003-09-30.abstract: Defectors, journal published by North Korean defectors, reports China purposely sent 58 North Koreans, once arrested for trying to leave China, on to North Korea to avoid negative international publicity; China still holds South Korean photographer Seok Jae Hyun, who was arrested documenting their attempted escape (S)

2003-09-30.title: China Angered by Reported Orgy Involving Japanese Tourists
2003-09-30.abstract: Police in China say they have detained six people in connection with assertions that hundreds of Japanese tourists hired similar numbers of local prostitutes and staged public display of promiscuity at luxury hotel in city of Zhuhai on sensitive anniversary in Sino-Japanese relations, day that China records as start of Japanese occupation of Manchuria in 1931 (M)

2003-09-30.title: Paris Journal; A Photo Op: O Say, Can You See the Eiffel Tower?
2003-09-30.abstract: Laura Bush's five-day trip to Paris and Moscow is considerd part of campaign to dispel criticism of Pres Bush's administration as unilateralist as it seeks to win international support for new United Nations resolution for more money and troops for American occupation of Iraq; she delivers 25-minute address inside headquarters of UNESCO, organization that US is rejoining after 19 years; photo (M)
